name: Security State Bank
description_short: Security State Bank Radcliffe, IA — Family-owned community bank operating since 1884 with 4 locations in Iowa, offering personal banking, business servi...
description_long:
Security State Bank is a family-owned and operated community bank originally established in Radcliffe, Iowa in 1884, with over 140 years of continuous service to local communities. The bank maintains its commitment to local decision-making and personalized customer relationships, operating under the philosophy of 'planting roots, not branches' rather than pursuing aggressive expansion. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit security for customers.

Security State Bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking products and services including personal banking (checking, savings, and deposit accounts), business banking solutions, online and mobile banking through their SSB Radcliffe app (available on App Store and Google Play), consumer loans, business loans, and credit card applications. The bank also provides access to insurance services through Drake Insurance & Financial Services. Customers can access their accounts from anywhere via secure online banking, and the bank offers multiple debit card design options. ATM access varies by location, with 24-hour walk-in or drive-up ATMs available at three of four branches.

What distinguishes Security State Bank is its deep local roots and community focus spanning over 140 years, combined with modern digital banking capabilities. The bank emphasizes local lending decisions and customer knowledge rather than corporate standardization. Their multi-location presence across four Iowa communities (Radcliffe, Hubbard, Story City, and Zearing) allows them to serve both rural and semi-rural markets. The integration of insurance services through Drake Insurance & Financial Services provides customers with broader financial protection options under one relationship.

Security State Bank is a legitimate community banking institution with proper FDIC insurance and established operations. However, as a small regional bank with only four locations in Iowa, it offers geographic limitations compared to national banks. The branch lobby hours are relatively limited (ending at 3-4:30pm on weekdays), which may inconvenience customers with standard work schedules. Prospective customers should verify that the bank's product offerings and rates meet their specific financial needs, as community banks may not always offer the full range of products or most competitive rates available from larger institutions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mobile banking app (SSB Radcliffe on iOS and Android)
Insurance services through Drake Insurance & Financial Services
ATM access (24-hour at Radcliffe, Story City; in-store at Hubbard; none at Zearing)
Deposit accounts and CDs (implied from banking services)
Account management with local customer service
pros: FDIC-insured with backing of full U.S. Government faith and credit
140+ years of continuous operation and family ownership demonstrating stability
Local lending decisions and emphasis on knowing customers personally
Mobile banking app available on both iOS and Android platforms
Four branch locations with extended Friday hours (until 5pm) and extended drive-up hours at Story City
24-hour ATM access available at three of four branch locations
Integrated insurance services through Drake Insurance & Financial Services
Multiple debit card design options available to customers
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only 4 locations in Iowa, restricting convenience for non-Iowa residents
Lobby hours end at 3-4:30pm Monday-Thursday, creating access challenges for working professionals
Zearing branch location has no ATM available on-site
As a small regional bank, likely offers fewer product options and potentially less competitive rates than national banks
Website content does not disclose specific interest rates, fees, or minimum balance requirements for accounts
best_for: Iowa residents in Radcliffe, Hubbard, Story City, or Zearing communities seeking personalized local banking
Small business owners preferring local lending decisions and community bank relationships
Customers who value 140+ years of institutional stability and family-owned operations
Individuals seeking FDIC-insured deposits with mobile and online banking convenience
diagnosis: Security State Bank is best for Iowa customers in its four-county service area who prioritize personal relationships and local decision-making over product breadth and competitive rates. The main caveat is that as a small regional bank, it has significant geographic limitations and may lack the full product suite or rate competitiveness of larger institutions—customers should verify specific offerings and rates match their needs before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
